memberdef.definition: using PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::R = typename GridR::continuous_dimension_type

briefdescription.para: The radial dimension.

memberdef.name: m_gko_matrix

memberdef.name: m_phi_spline_coef_alloc

memberdef.definition: FieldMem<double, IdxRangeQuadratureRTheta> PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::m_int_volume_alloc
memberdef.name: m_int_volume_alloc

memberdef.name: m_assembler

memberdef.definition: PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver
memberdef.argsstring: (Mapping const &mapping, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound const &spline_evaluator, std::optional< int > max_iter=std::nullopt, std::optional< double > res_tol=std::nullopt, std::optional< bool > batch_solver_logger=std::nullopt, std::optional< int > preconditioner_max_block_size=std::nullopt)
memberdef.name: PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver

briefdescription.para: Instantiate a polar Poisson-like solver using FEM with B-splines.

parameterdescription.para: The mapping from the logical domain to the physical domain where the equation is defined.
An evaluator for evaluating 2D splines on $(r,\theta)$.
The maximum number of iterations possible for the batched CSR solver.
The residual tolerance for the batched CSR solver. Be careful! the relative residual provided here, will be used as "implicit residual" in ginkgo solver.
Indicates whether log information such as the residual and the number of iterations should be monitored.
The maximum size of the Jacobi preconditioner used by the batched CSR solver.
A class describing a mapping from curvilinear coordinates to Cartesian coordinates.

memberdef.definition: void PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::update_coefficients
memberdef.name: update_coefficients

briefdescription.para: Update the coefficients $ alpha $ and $ beta $ that define the equation.

parameterdescription.para: The spline representation of the $ \alpha $ function in the definition of the Poisson-like equation.
The spline representation of the $ \beta $ function in the definition of the Poisson-like equation.

memberdef.definition: void PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::operator()
memberdef.name: operator()

briefdescription.para: Solve the Poisson-like equation.

detaileddescription.para: This operator returns the coefficients associated with the B-Splines of the solution $\phi$.

parameterdescription.para: The rhs $ \rho$ of the Poisson-like equation. The type is templated but we can use the PoissonLikeRHSFunction class. It must be an object with an operator() which evaluates a CoordRTheta and can be called from GPU.
The spline representation of the solution $\phi$.

memberdef.definition: void PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::operator()
memberdef.name: operator()

briefdescription.para: Solve the Poisson-like equation.

detaileddescription.para: This operator uses the other operator () and returns the values on the grid of the solution $\phi$.

parameterdescription.para: The rhs $ \rho$ of the Poisson-like equation. The type is templated but we can use the PoissonLikeRHSFunction class. It must be an object with an operator() which evaluates a CoordRTheta and can be called from GPU.
The values of the solution $\phi$ on the given coords_eval.

memberdef.definition: static KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION double PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::get_polar_bspline_vals
memberdef.name: get_polar_bspline_vals

briefdescription.para: Get the value of the specified polar bspline at the specified point.

parameterdescription.para: The coordinate where the value of the polar bspline should be calculated.
The polar bspline of interest.

simplesect.para: The value of the polar bspline at the coordinate.

memberdef.definition: static FieldMem< double, IdxRangeQuadratureRTheta > PolarSplineFEMPoissonLikeSolver< GridR, GridTheta, PolarBSplinesRTheta, SplineRThetaEvaluatorNullBound, Mapping, IdxRangeFull >::calculate_int_volume
memberdef.name: calculate_int_volume

briefdescription.para: Define a polar PDE solver for a Poisson-like equation.

detaileddescription.para: Solve the following Partial Differential Equation

As finite element basis functions we will use polar b-splines which are divided into two types: 1) Basis splines that can be written as a tensor product of 1d basis splines ("non-singular B-splines") 2) Basis splines that cover the centre point and are defined as a linear combination of basis splines of type 1 ("singular B-splines")
(see in Emily Bourne's thesis "Non-Uniform Numerical Schemes for the Modelling of Turbulence in the 5D GYSELA Code". December 2022.)

parameterdescription.para: The radial grid type.
The poloidal grid type.
The type of the 2D polar B-splines (on the coordinate system $(r,\theta)$ including B-splines which traverse the O point).
The type of the 2D (cross-product) spline evaluator.
The type of the mapping from the logical domain to the physical domain where the equation is defined.
The full index range of $ \phi $ including any batch dimensions.
